trivia_bullets.text: Walking Sarcophagus is set on Amonkhet, one of the three planes in Aetherdrift's Grand Prix circuit. Post-Phyrexian-invasion Amonkhet saw a "Walking Curse" raise tens of thousands of newly dead; these fresh undead resisted the old order of servile mummification and negotiated with the living — "Free us, and we will see Naktamun preserved" — forming an alliance that defines the plane's current state. [Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift Part 2]

Flavor text (Scryfall): "The warrior rose in a very different world from the one he'd left behind." — maps directly onto Amonkhet's Walking Curse narrative: an ancient mummified warrior (likely entombed under Nicol Bolas's reign) rises into a post-Bolas, post-Phyrexian Amonkhet that has been fundamentally transformed. The disorientation is lore-accurate: Bolas-era mummies were conditioned for servile purpose; what they awaken into is a plane rebuilding itself under living-and-dead cooperation. [Scryfall; Wizards: Planeswalker's Guide to Aetherdrift Part 2]

Scryfall confirms the type_line as "Artifact Creature — Zombie Cat" — an unusual creature type combination. Walking Sarcophagus carries the Start your engines! mechanic (Aetherdrift's signature speed-tracking keyword) with the Max speed bonus of +1/+2, turning it from a 2/1 into a 3/3 at max speed. The mechanic's design was called "enter the race" during vision design before being renamed [Wizards: You Get the Aetherdrift Part 1]. A 3/3 for {2} at max speed is a strong rate at common. [Scryfall oracle_text and type_line; Wizards: You Get the Aetherdrift Part 1]

Artist Julia Metzger rendered the card as a massive leonine stone statue rising from desert ruins — visually evoking both Egyptian sarcophagus imagery and Amonkhet's sphinx and guardian aesthetic. The card's name "Walking Sarcophagus" is a literal description: the mummified warrior is the coffin, now animate. The background includes a partially visible curved metal element (likely a race vehicle or track barrier) anchoring the scene in the Grand Prix world despite the ancient-ruins setting. [Scryfall artist field; card art as described in Vision pass]
